title: caryophyllidae
intro: The caryophyllidae is a superorder of flowering plants, typically comprising orders that have five-merous flowers, often with a distinct calyx and corolla. It includes a diverse range of families such as Caryophyllaceae, Polygonaceae, and Amaranthaceae. Examples of orders within the caryophyllidae superorder are Asparagales, Malpighiales, and Poales. These orders are characterized by their unique morphological and anatomical features, and often include plants with adaptations for drought tolerance, wind pollination, or other specialized traits.

orders.order_desc: Caryophyllales is a large and diverse order of flowering plants within the subclass Caryophyllidae of the class Magnoliopsida, consisting of over 25,000 species of plants including carnations, primroses, and pinks.
